title: tarenna pavettoides subsp. friesiorum
intro: Tarenna pavettoides subsp. friesiorum is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is native to Madagascar and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of Tarenna pavettoides subsp. friesiorum can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
